title: 5.5: Right Triangle Trigonometry

examples.text: Example \(\PageIndex{1}\) \(\alpha\)\(\alpha\)Given the triangle shown, find the value for \(\text{cos}(\alpha)\). The side adjacent to the angle is 15, and the hypotenuse of the triangle is 17, so \[\text{cos}(\alpha) = \dfrac{\text{adjacent}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \dfrac{15}{17}\nonumber\]

Example \(\PageIndex{3}\) Find the unknown sides of the triangle pictured here. Solution Since \(	ext{sin} (	heta) = \dfrac{	ext{opposite}}{	ext{hypotenuse}}\). \(	ext{sin}(30^{\circ}) = \dfrac{7}{b}\). From this, we can solve for the side \(b\). \[b\sin (30{}^\circ )=7
onumber\] \[b=\dfrac{7}{\sin (30{}^\circ )}
onumber\] To obtain a value, we can evaluate the sine and simplify \[b = \dfrac{7}{1/2} = 14
onumber\] To find the value for side \(a\), we could use the cosine, or simply apply the Pythagorean Theorem: \[a^2 + 7^2 = b^2
onumber\] \[a^2 + 7^2 = 14^2
onumber\] \[a = \sqrt{147}
onumber\]
